EmbeddedText.Text: You have no space to carry that!
Cannot claim home site while on a mission.
You have too many outposts for this site.

EmbeddedText.Notes: Used to localize dates. arg 1 is month, arg 2 is day, arg 3 is year

EmbeddedText.Text: Play as new user? You will return to the title screen without saving.
If no storage device is selected no save game will take place and all progress will be lost.
Choose a storage device to create new save data or load another save, or you may continue without saving but will lose any progress made.
There is more than one save game available. Please select the storage device with the save you want to use, or continue without saving losing any progress made.
Starting a new game will overwrite your existing save game and you will lose any progress made. Are you sure you want to start a new game?
Save Game Exists On This Storage Device
Would you like to load the existing save game?
Do you want to load this save, overwrite the save, or choose another storage device?
A save game cannot be found on this storage device. You can create a save on this storage device, choose another storage device, or continue without saving losing any progress made.
Reinsert the storage device, or you may continue without saving and lose any progress made.
A new save file has been created
Are you sure you want to overwrite this save game? All previous save data will be lost.
The selected storage device does not have enough space
The save game appears to be damaged and cannot be loaded. You can choose another storage device or continue without saving losing any progress made.
The disk appears to be corrupt
Are you sure you wish to delete this save?
Are you sure you wish to delete this save?
Deleting a saved game will destroy all progress in this save slot. The slot will be available for a new game. Are you sure?
This game supports an autosave feature. When you see this icon please do not turn off your console or remove the storage device.
This game supports an autosave feature. When you see this icon please do not turn off your pc or remove the storage device.
Export save games to cloud storage
While you see the save icon please do not turn off your console.
You are not connected to Xbox Live. Your scores will not be recorded to the leaderboards while disconnected.
A sign in change occurred. You are now being returned to the title screen.
Achievements are not available while not signed in.
Leaderboards are not available while not signed in.
You must be signed-in to Xbox Live in order to purchase the full game.
You must be signed-in to Xbox Live in order to purchase Breakdown.
Are you sure you want to exit? Any unsaved progress will be lost.
Do you want to sign in? If you don't you will be unable to save progress.
Welcome to State of Decay! Join your fellow survivors at forums.undeadlabs.com for game tips and news!

EmbeddedText.Notes: Facility needs a staff member assigned to it

EmbeddedText.Text: Choice is final for each character. Hold [ui_x] to choose.

EmbeddedText.Notes: Used for item weights at least a pound
Used for item weights less than a pound
Used for item weights less than 0.01 pounds
Indicates that the character is an advisor
param is the maxium count of resources that can be stored

EmbeddedText.Text: View your possession lists here - weapons, guns, automobiles and items

EmbeddedText.Notes: Notification that plays when the player's current weapon gets damaged

EmbeddedText.Text: Leaderboards are not available while not connected to Xbox Live.
You are not ranked on this leaderboard.
Your friends are not ranked on this leaderboard.
Nobody is ranked on this leaderboard.
Items added to the supply locker!
Add %1$s as a FRIEND. Friends share Fame and Influence. You can switch between friends when you aren't on a mission.
I'm beat. Why don't you take over for awhile?
Switch to %1$s and let %2$s rest. %2$s will regain maximum Vitality and Stamina while resting.
Come with me and watch my back.
Have %s accompany you and fight with you. (%d Influence)
No room for %s on the RV.

EmbeddedText.Notes: Warning message displayed when players stray from a mission area

EmbeddedText.Text: Unlock the full game to receive it.
STORAGE facilities increase your maximum storage capacity.
There's no point in stockpiling more resources than we can store properly. -Lily
RESOURCES are gathered and consumed when you are away for a short period of time.
Bonus RESOURCES are gathered from OUTPOSTS when you are away for more than a day provided you have materials for upkeep.
Survivors need 1 Material to maintain an Outpost. In exchange, they return with 3 of whichever resource the Outpost contains.
The brass have given %1$s more operational discretion.

State of Decay is an open sandbox world that develops in real time, dynamically responding to your actions, the choices you make, and the ever-increasing zombie threat. Decide where you'd like to set up a fortified safe haven for you and other survivors you've rescued, then scout for critical supplies and call runners to gather resources and create supply chains. Use the materials you've gathered to build and upgrade your home base, but be careful! Resources will deplete as you pillage areas, and zombies are attracted to noise and activity. Take up the offensive by assaulting hordes before they reach your home, or focus on improving your defenses against the massed zombie attacks. Every choice and every action matter in this fully simulated, evolving world.

Manage your Home via your Journal. Here you can see the status of all your community members, including their Vitality, Stamina, inventory, skills, current condition, and interesting character traits. Earning the friendship of other survivors unlocks the ability to play as them. Help ensure your long-term survival by scavenging supplies, rescuing more survivors to join your community, and building facilities that enable you to create your own resources. Every base has a unique combination of built-in facilities as well as space to build your own. Feeling cramped? Need room to grow? Seek out new locations that offer the protection and space your community needs to expand. Keep an eye on your stockpiles as the strength of your supplies directly impacts your community's morale. Low morale paves the way to negative consequences.

Navigate the World: Press [xi_a] to interact with a range of environmental objects, from climbing fences, hurdling barriers, to crashing through windows. Stealth: Hold [xi_b] to crouch and enter stealth mode. Use the [xi_thumblx] to move while crouched. Tap [xi_b] again to exit crouch. Crouching while in bushes provides additional stealth. Sneak up on wandering zombies to deliver an instantaneous killing blow with [sprint] + [xi_use]. Discovery: Use [xi_y] to interact with objects in the world such as opening doors, searching containers, and barricading windows. Express Yourself: Experiment with the effects of the different emotes your character can display. Hold [emote_hold] then press [xi_y], [xi_b], [xi_a], or [xi_x].

Practice makes perfect. Every character in the world has a unique set of skills and traits, some of which can impact combat effectiveness. Improve skills through use, then choose which specializations to unlock. Are you a stealth expert, able to sneak into any building? Or are you a one-shot zombie killing machine? Contextual Moves: Press [sprint] + [xi_use] for interactive moves such as pushing a zombie away or shoving it against a wall. Finishers: When an enemy is in a prone position, press [sprint] + [xi_use] to perform a finishing move.

Like all valuable resources in the world, cars are in limited supply. You'll want to get them home and fixed up in your workshop to keep them in top working order. If a vehicle gets damaged beyond repair, it's gone forever. And don't forget, vehicles are noisy!

Keep an eye on your map and your ears tuned to the radio for new mission opportunities. Mission types range in variety, from scavenging runs to helping your neighbors fight off a zombie attack. Decide which missions to take based on your community's needs. Some mission opportunities are time sensitive, so choose your priorities carefully. Survival is exhausting work and, unlike zombies, people need to rest. When your Vitality and Stamina are running low, head back to your home base to rest up. Maybe take another community member out for a stroll.

Cash is no longer a factor in the post-apocalyptic zombie world. The new currency is supplies and deeds that earn you Influence. Influence plays a large role in getting what you want, whether it be ammo from your own community's supply locker or borrowing some meds from your neighbors. Earning Influence is pretty straightforward: Get Shit Done.

EmbeddedText.Notes: Move to next page in 'how to play' menu
Move to previous page in 'how to play' menu

EmbeddedText.Text: Breakdown is a new, open-ended, endless mode for State of Decay. There is no scripted storyline. Everything is based on your choices. When you exhaust your resources, you can climb aboard an RV, and head out to a new scenario with replenished supplies ... and more difficult zombies.

When you begin your first Breakdown game, you will need to find a community to join. You have multiple communities to choose from, each marked on the map. When you find the one you want to join, simply speak to their leader (who is standing precisely under the map marker), and announce that you want to join up.

Even before you are ready to move on to the next map, you'll want to keep your eyes open for a working RV propped up on a jack, somewhere in the environment. That RV is your ticket out. When you approach it for the first time, it is added to the Home Tab in your Journal, next to the Outposts. From then on, you can use that RV icon to repair, load up, and leave in the RV.
Your RV eventually breaks down in an area with refreshed supplies where you can start to rebuild. It will serve as a temporary home site for you until you can find a new place to live.

Each time you move on in the RV, your difficulty level increases. You can check your current difficulty level by visiting the Challenge tab in the Journal. A high difficulty level means denser, faster, more dangerous zombies, as well as reductions to available resources. Once a difficulty level has been unlocked, you can select it as your starting point in any future Breakdown game.

The Challenge Tab in your Journal displays a list of Challenges, each of which is tied to a particular Hero. Complete a challenge, and its Hero will shortly appear as a recruitable character, and will begin to show up randomly in the world in all your future Breakdown games. You may also select that Hero as your starting character when beginning a new game from scratch. Heroes usually begin with upgraded skills, unique abilities, and special weapons.

Zombie kills, feats of fighting skill, completed missions, Home upgrades, and a range of other accomplishments all contribute to the Score that you earn at the end of a Breakdown scenario. Whenever you leave the map (or die), your Score will be displayed, and that same Score will be tracked and recorded on the Breakdown leaderboards. Playing at a higher difficulty has a major positive impact on your Score.

Navigate the World: Press [jump] to interact with a range of environmental objects, from climbing fences, hurdling barriers, to crashing through windows. Stealth: Hold [cover] to crouch and enter stealth mode. Use [moveforward], [moveleft], [moveback], [moveright] to move while crouched. . Tap [cover] again to exit crouch. Crouching while in bushes provides additional stealth. Sneak up on wandering zombies to deliver an instantaneous killing blow. Discovery: Use [xi_use] to interact with objects in the world such as opening doors, searching containers, and barricading windows. Express Yourself: Experiment with the effects of the different emotes your character can display. Press [emote1], [emote4], [emote3], or [emote2] to emote.

Practice makes perfect. Every character in the world has a unique set of skills and traits, some of which can impact combat effectiveness. Improve skills through use, then choose which specializations to unlock. Are you a stealth expert, able to sneak into any building? Or are you a one-shot zombie killing machine? Contextual Moves: Press [execute] for interactive moves such as pushing a zombie away or shoving it against a wall. Finishers: When an enemy is in a prone position, press [execute] to perform a finishing move.

State of Decay: Lifeline is a military-themed expansion to State of Decay, featuring a new map, new characters, and new ways to play. You are Greyhound One, a U.S. Army unit that has been inserted into a zombie-infested city to rescue key personnel, starting with a research scientist named Dr. Thomas Horn, whose helicopter crashed just outside of town.

The city of Danforth is almost completely overrun with zombies. Press [ul_map_menu] to view the map. The red areas are Danger Zones. One step inside a Danger Zone will attract a swarm of zombies from the urban center. Use the elevated highway to navigate the outskirts of the city, and stay out of harm's way ... mostly.

Once you have established your base of operations, press [ul_journal_menu] to manage your facilities. New additions for Lifeline include Defenses (traps and distractions), the Landing Zone (supply drops), the Ops Center (tactics and research), the Generator (unlocking advanced facility actions), and the Latrine (staving off sickness). Make sure to explore all the new and advanced options at your disposal.

You were sent to Danforth specifically to rescue High Value Targets (marked with RED icons on the map). These are the most important missions to complete. Fail or ignore too many of them, and your operation will be scrapped. Civilians (WHITE icons) and Soldiers (GREEN icons) are also valuable, but not mission-critical. Rescued Soldiers may become playable members of your unit. High Value Targets and Civilians will not. When rescued, they will be extracted on the next helicopter.

The current Threat Level (displayed below the mini-map) indicates how soon your base will be hit by a siege -- a major zombie attack. This threat builds up over time as zombies escape the city, and then an incoming helicopter from HQ will draw them straight to your base. This means that when you rescue a High Value Target or a Civilian, they will need to survive a siege before they can be airlifted to safety. Whenever a siege is inbound, get back to your base quickly to help defend it, or people are likely to die.

Press [ul_radio_menu] to access radio options from HQ, including artillery strikes, reinforcements, and drone recon (revealing zombie and building locations on the map) ... plus the ability to place Outposts. Eventually, you may need to establish your own access to these abilities by searching for appropriate military sites around the city (Drone Trailers, Artillery Sites, Satellite Uplinks), and placing Outposts there.

All versions of State of Decay have now been upgraded with Vehicle Storage. Approach the rear of any driveable vehicle, and press [xi_use] to access the inventory for that vehicle. You may load conventional inventory items (weapons, ammunition, consumables, etc) or rucksack resources (Food, Ammo, Materials, Medicine, Fuel) into a vehicle. When you drive a vehicle into your Parking facility, any rucksack resources it contains are automatically delivered into your base.
Difficulty Level determines how hard and how rewarding
Play as a random non-hero character. At first, this is your only option. After heroes are unlocked, you can still choose it for an extra challenge!
Please restart the game to switch to this game mode.
Fewer resources. Fewer cars. More fast zombies.
Fewer resources. Fewer cars. More fast zombies.
Fewer resources. Fewer cars. More fast zombies.
Fewer resources. Fewer cars. More fast zombies.
Fewer resources. Fewer cars. More fast zombies.
Fewer resources. Fewer cars. More fast zombies.
Fewer resources. Fewer cars. More fast zombies.
Fewer resources. Fewer cars. More fast zombies.
Small extra damage. No map indicators.
Screamers! Extra Screamers. Small extra damage. No map indicators.
Small extra damage. No map indicators.
Bulletproof! Extra armored zombies. Small extra damage. No map indicators.
Small extra damage. No map indicators.
Too Quiet... Extra ambush zombies. Small extra damage. No map indicators.
Small extra damage. No map indicators.
Juggernauts! Extra Juggernauts. Lethal Juggernauts (grabs kill, regardless of health). Small extra damage. No map indicators.
Moderate extra damage. Lethal Juggernauts. No map indicators.
Biohazard! Extra Bloaters. Moderate extra damage. Lethal Juggernauts. No map indicators.
Moderate extra damage. Lethal Juggernauts. No map indicators.
Dead Man's Party! Extra hordes. Extra infestations. Moderate extra damage. Lethal Juggernauts. No map indicator.
Moderate extra damage. Lethal Juggernauts. No map indicators.
Creeping Death. Extra Screamers. Extra Bloaters. Moderate extra damage. Lethal Juggernauts. No map indicators.
Moderate extra damage. Lethal Juggernauts. No map indicators.
Frenzy! Extra Ferals. Lethal Juggernauts. Moderate extra damage. No map indicators.
Moderate extra damage. Lethal Juggernauts. No map indicators.
Tooth and Claw. Extra Ferals. Extra Juggernauts. Lethal Juggernauts. Moderate extra damage. No Map indicators.
Armageddon. Scariest environment imaginable. Massive extra damage. Lethal Juggernauts. No map indicators.
Armageddon. Scariest environment imaginable. Massive extra damage. Lethal Juggernauts. No map indicators.
Armageddon. Scariest environment imaginable. Massive extra damage. Lethal Juggernauts. No map indicators.
Moving on will reset your Challenge progress. You're REALLY starting over. Are you sure you want to leave now?
[%s] is not allowed for this action.
REWARD. A collection of weapons for the zombie-slaying sportsman, delivered directly to your Supply Locker.
REWARD. A porcine tow truck, delivered near your current location.
REWARD. An attractively branded sportscar, delivered near your current location.
REWARD. A rare incendiary revolver, delivered directly to your Supply Locker.
REWARD. A character who specializes in demolition, added immediately to your community.
REWARD. A collection of heavy tools used by fire and rescue services, delivered directly to your Supply Locker.
REWARD. A distinctive large backpack, delivered directly to your Supply Locker.
REWARD. A collection of masterfully-crafted blades, delivered directly to your Supply Locker.
REWARD. Delivers a crateload of Pie to your supply locker, and unlocks a new Facility Action in the Kitchen.
REWARD. A high-capacity firearm and a collection of quality suppressors, delivered directly to your Supply Locker.
REWARD. Someone in your community creates a celebratory weapon to mark the new year. No one will admit it was them.
REWARD. A beautiful high-capacity firearm, decorated to express the love and devotion that your community members feel towards one another.
REWARD. Several rucksacks' worth of Ammunition, delivered directly into your stockpile.
REWARD. Several rucksacks' worth of Medicine, delivered directly into your stockpile.
REWARD. Several rucksacks' worth of Food, delivered directly into your stockpile.
REWARD. Several rucksacks' worth of Fuel, delivered directly into your stockpile.
REWARD. Thank you for purchasing the original State of Decay! Select this option, and an exclusive character will be added directly to your community.
Thank you for supporting the original State of Decay! To show our thanks, we've unlocked a UNIQUE CHARACTER for you!

EmbeddedText.Notes: If we manage to get Gurubani selectable on the front end.
EmbeddedText.Text: You'll find GURUBANI KAUR on the Hero Menu in Breakdown. Or use the Radio Menu to add her after you've established a base.

EmbeddedText.Notes: If we DON'T manage to get Gurubani selectable on the front end.
EmbeddedText.Text: Use the Radio Menu to add GURUBANI KAUR to your community after you've established a base.
Once you have established your base in State of Decay, Breakdown, or Lifeline, you can use your RADIO to call in a unique supply drop that includes an SUV, a Folding Axe, and an Assault Rifle. Enjoy!
We've detected a transferred save from the original State of Decay.
Do you want to use your original saved game, or delete it and continue normally?
We can export a copy of your current saved game to be loaded into State of Decay: Year-One Survival Edition on Xbox One.
You are about to import your Xbox 360 save. This will overwrite and destroy any State of Decay save data already on this device. Are you sure you want to proceed?
The import process failed for some reason. You may be suffering connectivity issues, or the file itself may be corrupted.
Warning! This will import your save from the original State of Decay, and will OVERWRITE any progress you have made in the Year-One Survival Edition. Are you sure you want to do this?
Success! Your save from the original State of Decay has been imported.
Oh no! Something went wrong. Please try again, or go to forums.undeadlabs.com if you need further assistance.
